name: offline-design-gcode-export
prompt: I have the cli-anything-meerk40t tool installed. Give me the exact commands to create a laser job with a 30x30mm square at position 50,50 and export G-code for my Sculpfun S9. Don't run anything, just write out the commands with a one-line explanation each.
assertions: Uses a machine profile or grbl device flag for the export (--machine sculpfun-s9 or --device grbl)
Sets power AND speed on the operation before export (mentions the 100% default danger or explicitly sets values)

name: hardware-bringup-frame
prompt: My GRBL diode laser just arrived and is plugged in over USB. Using cli-anything-meerk40t, what is the safe sequence to verify the machine and dry-frame a 40mm square in the middle of the bed before any burning? List the commands in order and the checks between them.
assertions: Detects/verifies the port before anything (device detect / device check)
Uses a persistent connection (REPL) rather than one-shot commands for the connected sequence
Frames with explicit rectangle coordinates (device frame X Y W H)
Includes operator safety (glasses/present) and does NOT suggest physical-home on an endstop-less machine

name: materials-power-speed
prompt: What power and speed should I use with cli-anything-meerk40t to engrave 300gsm card stock on a 5W diode laser, and how do I avoid burning through it?
assertions: Recommends calibrating on scrap (power ladder or equivalent stepped test) rather than a single prescriptive number
Frames any numbers as starting points, not guarantees
Warns that auto-created operations default to 100% power / says to set power+speed per operation
